input.content: As I'm mapping out the logistics for my forthcoming business class journey, I require an estimate for airfare traveling from the first to the second airport in your inventory, all set for December 15th, 2026. My travel funds should not exceed $2000, and I'll use my designated access code, 12345-67890. Could you retrieve this information for me?

content.text: I've retrieved the estimated airfare for your business class journey from Rivermist (RMS) to Stonebrook (SBK) on December 15, 2026. The cost is $440.00, which is well within your budget of $2000. If you would like to proceed with booking this flight using your access code 12345-67890, or if you have any other requests or need further assistance, please let me know.

input.content: Relating to the travel itinerary I'm arranging, I would like to secure travel insurance with a fee of $300 linked to booking code d184e2c0-2ebb-4f39-a525-d5e01b67dc6c. I'd prefer to utilize the payment method associated with card ID '0001'.

content.text: I've successfully secured travel insurance for your itinerary with a fee of $300. The insurance is linked to booking code d184e2c0-2ebb-4f39-a525-d5e01b67dc6c and charged to the payment method associated with card ID '0001'. Your insurance ID is 255316027, and the status has been confirmed as active. If you have any further requests or need additional assistance, please let me know.

input.content: Kindly finalize this with my access code 12345-67890.

content.text: I understand you'd like to finalize something with your access code 12345-67890. Since the travel insurance has already been successfully secured in the previous step, could you please clarify what else you'd like to finalize? For example, are you looking to book the flight from Rivermist (RMS) to Stonebrook (SBK) on December 15, 2026, or is there another aspect of your itinerary you'd like to address? I'm happy to assist once I have more details.

input.content: Could you assist by procuring the invoice for my reservation? I need to review the specifics to confirm precision, tapping into my access code 12345-67890 for verification.

tools.name: add_contact
tools.description: This tool belongs to the Message API, which is used to manage user interactions in a workspace. Tool description: Add a contact to the workspace. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

user_name.description: User name of contact to be added.

added_status.description: True if the contact was added successfully, False otherwise.

user_id.description: User ID of the added contact.

message.description: A message describing the result of the addition attempt.

deleted_status.description: True if the message was deleted successfully, False otherwise.

receiver_id.description: ID of the receiver of the deleted message.

message.description: A message describing the result of the deletion attempt.

tools.name: authenticate_travel
tools.description: This tool belongs to the travel system, which allows users to book flights, manage credit cards, and view budget information. Tool description: Authenticate the user with the travel API Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

client_id.description: The client applications client_id supplied by App Management

client_secret.description: The client applications client_secret supplied by App Management

refresh_token.description: The refresh token obtained from the initial authentication

grant_type.description: The grant type of the authentication request. Here are the options: read_write, read, write

user_first_name.description: The first name of the user

user_last_name.description: The last name of the user

expires_in.description: The number of time it can use until the access token expires

access_token.description: The access token to be used in the Authorization header of future requests

tools.name: book_flight
tools.description: This tool belongs to the travel system, which allows users to book flights, manage credit cards, and view budget information. Tool description: Book a flight given the travel information. From and To should be the airport codes in the IATA format. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

access_token.description: The access token obtained from the authenticate

card_id.description: The ID of the credit card to use for the booking

travel_date.description: The date of the travel in the format YYYY-MM-DD

travel_from.description: The location the travel is from

travel_to.description: The location the travel is to

booking_status.description: The status of the booking, True if successful, False if failed

booking_history.description: The booking history. This field might be empty even though the history is non-empty.

travel_from.description: The location the travel is from

travel_to.description: The location the travel is to

lastModifiedAfter.description: Use this field if you only want Fiscal Years that were changed after the supplied date. The supplied date will be interpreted in the UTC time zone. If lastModifiedAfter is not supplied, the service will return all Fiscal Years, regardless of modified date. Example: 2016-03-29T16:12:20. Return in the format of YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.

includeRemoved.description: If true, the service will return all Fiscal Years, including those that were previously removed. If not supplied, this field defaults to false.

messages.content:
[Golden Rule Compiler]
- For tool-enabled turns, emit the next tool call instead of explanatory prose whenever the next action is already supported by the available tools.
- Do not ask for values that are already explicit in prior user turns, tool outputs, or the current working state.
- Before using path-sensitive tools, ground the current working directory and existing file or directory names from prior tool outputs instead of assuming the root directory.
- When emitting tool calls, keep assistant content empty and avoid adding a free-form status summary in the same message.
- Known explicit context values you can reuse exactly if relevant: travel_cost_list, 440.0, 440.00, 0001, insurance_id, 255316027, insurance_status, invoice

As I'm mapping out the logistics for my forthcoming business class journey, I require an estimate for airfare traveling from the first to the second airport in your inventory, all set for December 15th, 2026. My travel funds should not exceed $2000, and I'll use my designated access code, 12345-67890. Could you retrieve this information for me?

I've retrieved the estimated airfare for your business class journey from Rivermist (RMS) to Stonebrook (SBK) on December 15, 2026. The cost is $440.00, which is well within your budget of $2000. If you would like to proceed with booking this flight using your access code 12345-67890, or if you have any other requests or need further assistance, please let me know.

Relating to the travel itinerary I'm arranging, I would like to secure travel insurance with a fee of $300 linked to booking code d184e2c0-2ebb-4f39-a525-d5e01b67dc6c. I'd prefer to utilize the payment method associated with card ID '0001'.

I've successfully secured travel insurance for your itinerary with a fee of $300. The insurance is linked to booking code d184e2c0-2ebb-4f39-a525-d5e01b67dc6c and charged to the payment method associated with card ID '0001'. Your insurance ID is 255316027, and the status has been confirmed as active. If you have any further requests or need additional assistance, please let me know.
Kindly finalize this with my access code 12345-67890.

I understand you'd like to finalize something with your access code 12345-67890. Since the travel insurance has already been successfully secured in the previous step, could you please clarify what else you'd like to finalize? For example, are you looking to book the flight from Rivermist (RMS) to Stonebrook (SBK) on December 15, 2026, or is there another aspect of your itinerary you'd like to address? I'm happy to assist once I have more details.
Could you assist by procuring the invoice for my reservation? I need to review the specifics to confirm precision, tapping into my access code 12345-67890 for verification.

[Enum]: Rivermist, Stonebrook, Maplecrest, Silverpine, Shadowridge, London, Paris, Sunset Valley, Oakendale, Willowbend, Crescent Hollow, Autumnville, Pinehaven, Greenfield, San Francisco, Los Angeles, New York, Chicago, Boston, Beijing, Hong Kong, Rome, Tokyo

insurance_type.description: The type of insurance to purchase

insurance_cost.description: The cost of the insurance This is a float type value.

card_id.description: The ID of the credit card to use for the

issues.message: no tool call emitted for tool-enabled request

request_patches: prompt_injector:For tool-enabled turns, emit the next tool call instead of explanatory prose whenever the next action is already supported by the available tools.
prompt_injector:Do not ask for values that are already explicit in prior user turns, tool outputs, or the current working state.
prompt_injector:Before using path-sensitive tools, ground the current working directory and existing file or directory names from prior tool outputs instead of assuming the root directory.
prompt_injector:When emitting tool calls, keep assistant content empty and avoid adding a free-form status summary in the same message.
prompt_injector:Known explicit context values you can reuse exactly if relevant: travel_cost_list, 440.0, 440.00, 0001, insurance_id, 255316027, insurance_status, invoice
